pattern: The main issue seems to be ...
b1: The main issue seems to be the lack of time.
The main issue seems to be the price of the product.
The main issue seems to be the noise in the office.
The main issue seems to be the bad weather.
The main issue seems to be that he is always late.
b2: The main issue seems to be the miscommunication between the two teams.
The main issue seems to be the outdated software we are using.
The main issue seems to be the high cost of maintenance.
The main issue seems to be the negative feedback from our customers.
The main issue seems to be the lack of resources for the new project.
c1: The main issue seems to be the inherent instability of the current market.
The main issue seems to be the systemic failure of the quality control process.
The main issue seems to be the discrepancy between the theoretical model and the empirical data.
The main issue seems to be the deep-seated cultural resistance to change within the organization.
The main issue seems to be the potential conflict of interest among the stakeholders.

pattern: How can we best approach ...?
translation: ...에 어떻게 가장 잘 접근할 수 있을까요?
b1: How can we best approach this problem?
How can we best approach our new neighbors?
How can we best approach the final exam?
How can we best approach the travel planning?
How can we best approach learning a new language?
b2: How can we best approach the negotiation with the supplier?
How can we best approach the task of restructuring the department?
How can we best approach the challenge of reducing our carbon footprint?
How can we best approach the launch of our new marketing campaign?
How can we best approach the difficult conversation with the client?
c1: How can we best approach the integration of AI into our existing workflows?
How can we best approach the sensitive issue of ethical implications in research?
How can we best approach the complex task of global brand repositioning?
How can we best approach the long-term sustainability of our business model?
How can we best approach the multi-faceted challenges of urban poverty?

pattern: I suggest that we ...
b1: I suggest that we take a short break.
I suggest that we go to the park together.
I suggest that we study more for the test.
I suggest that we buy a new printer.
I suggest that we talk to the teacher.
b2: I suggest that we implement a more flexible work schedule.
I suggest that we conduct a survey to get customer feedback.
I suggest that we invest more in employee training programs.
I suggest that we revise the current budget plan for next year.
I suggest that we hire a professional consultant to help us.
c1: I suggest that we adopt a more proactive approach to risk management.
I suggest that we leverage our existing data to optimize the supply chain.
I suggest that we re-evaluate our long-term objectives in light of current trends.
I suggest that we establish a multi-disciplinary task force to address the issue.
I suggest that we foster a culture of transparency and accountability.

pattern: A potential solution is to ...
b1: A potential solution is to ask for help.
A potential solution is to save more money.
A potential solution is to buy a better lock.
A potential solution is to walk instead of driving.
A potential solution is to practice every day.
b2: A potential solution is to automate the data entry process.
A potential solution is to offer discounts to loyal customers.
A potential solution is to relocate the office to a more central area.
A potential solution is to simplify the user interface of our app.
A potential solution is to establish a more rigorous screening process.
c1: A potential solution is to diversify our investment portfolio to mitigate risks.
A potential solution is to implement a decentralized decision-making system.
A potential solution is to integrate advanced analytics into our strategic planning.
A potential solution is to overhaul the entire regulatory framework governing this industry.
A potential solution is to foster collaborative partnerships with our competitors.

pattern: What if we tried ... instead of ...?
b1: What if we tried tea instead of coffee?
What if we tried walking instead of taking the bus?
What if we tried studying at night instead of in the morning?
What if we tried the red one instead of the blue one?
What if we tried a different restaurant instead of this one?
b2: What if we tried digital marketing instead of traditional advertising?
What if we tried a collaborative approach instead of a competitive one?
What if we tried hiring freelancers instead of full-time employees?
What if we tried a subscription model instead of a one-time purchase?
What if we tried a more informal tone instead of a professional one?
c1: What if we tried a qualitative assessment instead of a purely quantitative one?
What if we tried an incremental implementation strategy instead of a total overhaul?
What if we tried leveraging open-source solutions instead of proprietary software?
What if we tried a bottom-up approach to management instead of a top-down one?
What if we tried focusing on niche markets instead of competing in the mass market?

pattern: We need to prioritize ...
b1: We need to prioritize safety.
We need to prioritize our health.
We need to prioritize the children's needs.
We need to prioritize finishing this task.
We need to prioritize saving water.
b2: We need to prioritize customer satisfaction over short-term profits.
We need to prioritize the most urgent tasks on the list.
We need to prioritize the development of new security features.
We need to prioritize effective communication within the team.
We need to prioritize the quality of the product over the speed of production.
c1: We need to prioritize the long-term sustainability of our environmental initiatives.
We need to prioritize the ethical implications of our data collection policies.
We need to prioritize the psychological well-being of our workforce.
We need to prioritize the allocation of resources to high-impact research areas.
We need to prioritize fiscal responsibility to ensure the firm's stability.

pattern: Let's break the problem down into ...
b1: Let's break the problem down into small steps.
Let's break the problem down into three parts.
Let's break the problem down into easy tasks.
Let's break the problem down into day and night work.
Let's break the problem down into groups.
b2: Let's break the problem down into manageable components.
Let's break the problem down into short-term and long-term goals.
Let's break the problem down into technical and administrative issues.
Let's break the problem down into different stages of production.
Let's break the problem down into individual responsibilities.
c1: Let's break the problem down into its fundamental constituent elements.
Let's break the problem down into discrete analytical categories.
Let's break the problem down into chronological sequences to identify the root cause.
Let's break the problem down into socio-economic and political factors.
Let's break the problem down into internal vulnerabilities and external threats.

pattern: Have you considered the impact of ...?
b1: Have you considered the impact of the rain?
Have you considered the impact of the noise?
Have you considered the impact of social media?
Have you considered the impact of your words?
Have you considered the impact of the new rules?
b2: Have you considered the impact of inflation on our project budget?
Have you considered the impact of remote work on employee productivity?
Have you considered the impact of the new competitor in the market?
Have you considered the impact of these changes on the local community?
Have you considered the impact of the technological shift on our industry?
c1: Have you considered the long-term environmental impact of the proposed factory?
Have you considered the potential socio-political impact of the new legislation?
Have you considered the cumulative impact of these minor technical glitches?
Have you considered the psychological impact of the restructuring on the staff?
Have you considered the economic impact of the global supply chain disruption?

pattern: One way around this is to ...
translation: 이를 해결하는 한 가지 방법은 ...하는 것입니다.
b1: One way around this is to use a map.
One way around this is to ask a friend.
One way around this is to try again later.
One way around this is to take a different road.
One way around this is to use a cheaper option.
b2: One way around this is to outsource the technical support.
One way around this is to negotiate a more flexible deadline.
One way around this is to use cloud-based storage instead of physical servers.
One way around this is to collaborate with a local partner.
One way around this is to simplify the requirements for the applicants.
c1: One way around this is to implement a modular design that allows for future scalability.
One way around this is to utilize a workaround that bypasses the legacy system.
One way around this is to establish a legal entity in another jurisdiction.
One way around this is to leverage existing open-source frameworks to reduce costs.
One way around this is to adopt a hybrid approach that combines both theories.

pattern: We could mitigate the risk by ...
b1: We could mitigate the risk by wearing a helmet.
We could mitigate the risk by checking the weather.
We could mitigate the risk by going slowly.
We could mitigate the risk by saving money.
We could mitigate the risk by being careful.
b2: We could mitigate the risk by diversifying our suppliers.
We could mitigate the risk by conducting thorough background checks.
We could mitigate the risk by purchasing comprehensive insurance.
We could mitigate the risk by setting up a backup server.
We could mitigate the risk by training staff on safety protocols.
c1: We could mitigate the risk by implementing a rigorous auditing system.
We could mitigate the risk by hedging our foreign exchange exposure.
We could mitigate the risk by incorporating contingency clauses into the contract.
We could mitigate the risk by conducting a series of pilot studies before the full launch.
We could mitigate the risk by establishing an independent oversight committee.

pattern: Perhaps we should look at the root cause of ...
translation: 어쩌면 ...의 근본 원인을 살펴봐야 할 것 같습니다.
b1: Perhaps we should look at the root cause of the noise.
Perhaps we should look at the root cause of the smell.
Perhaps we should look at the root cause of the mistake.
Perhaps we should look at the root cause of the fight.
Perhaps we should look at the root cause of the broken toy.
b2: Perhaps we should look at the root cause of the high employee turnover.
Perhaps we should look at the root cause of the declining sales figures.
Perhaps we should look at the root cause of the frequent system failures.
Perhaps we should look at the root cause of the misunderstanding between the teams.
Perhaps we should look at the root cause of the budget overruns.
c1: Perhaps we should look at the root cause of the systemic inefficiencies within the department.
Perhaps we should look at the root cause of the psychological barriers to innovation.
Perhaps we should look at the root cause of the recurring socio-economic disparities.
Perhaps we should look at the root cause of the discrepancy in the experimental data.
Perhaps we should look at the root cause of the cultural friction during the merger.

pattern: The immediate next step should be ...
b1: The immediate next step should be to call the police.
The immediate next step should be to clean the floor.
The immediate next step should be to find a map.
The immediate next step should be to say sorry.
The immediate next step should be to eat lunch.
b2: The immediate next step should be to notify all the stakeholders about the delay.
The immediate next step should be to conduct an emergency meeting with the board.
The immediate next step should be to fix the bug in the system.
The immediate next step should be to gather more data on customer behavior.
The immediate next step should be to secure additional funding for the project.
c1: The immediate next step should be to initiate a comprehensive forensic audit of the finances.
The immediate next step should be to establish a crisis management protocol.
The immediate next step should be to issue a formal statement to the press.
The immediate next step should be to assess the legal ramifications of the breach.
The immediate next step should be to stabilize the primary infrastructure to prevent further loss.

pattern: Does anyone have a different perspective on ...?
translation: ...에 대해 다른 의견이 있는 분 계신가요?
b1: Does anyone have a different perspective on this movie?
Does anyone have a different perspective on the food?
Does anyone have a different perspective on the music?
Does anyone have a different perspective on the game?
Does anyone have a different perspective on this plan?
b2: Does anyone have a different perspective on the new office policy?
Does anyone have a different perspective on the results of the survey?
Does anyone have a different perspective on the proposed marketing strategy?
Does anyone have a different perspective on the impact of technology in schools?
Does anyone have a different perspective on how to handle the customer's complaint?
c1: Does anyone have a different perspective on the ethical considerations of the project?
Does anyone have a different perspective on the theoretical framework we are using?
Does anyone have a different perspective on the socio-economic implications of the crisis?
Does anyone have a different perspective on the validity of the research methodology?
Does anyone have a different perspective on the long-term strategic direction of the firm?

pattern: In the short term, we can do ...
b1: In the short term, we can do more exercise.
In the short term, we can save some money.
In the short term, we can buy a new phone.
In the short term, we can wait for the bus.
In the short term, we can use a fan.
b2: In the short term, we can reduce our unnecessary expenses.
In the short term, we can hire a temporary assistant to help with the workload.
In the short term, we can focus on improving our current product line.
In the short term, we can implement a temporary fix for the software bug.
In the short term, we can increase our presence on social media.
c1: In the short term, we can implement cost-cutting measures to preserve liquidity.
In the short term, we can utilize a tactical approach to address the immediate crisis.
In the short term, we can reallocate internal resources to the most critical tasks.
In the short term, we can provide temporary relief to the affected communities.
In the short term, we can initiate a pilot program to test the new strategy.

pattern: We should implement a system for ...
b1: We should implement a system for cleaning the house.
We should implement a system for saving water.
We should implement a system for recycling trash.
We should implement a system for doing homework.

We should implement a system for fostering inter-departmental collaboration.
We should implement a system for the continuous professional development of our staff.

pattern: Let's evaluate the pros and cons of ...
b1: Let's evaluate the pros and cons of living in a city.
Let's evaluate the pros and cons of having a dog.
Let's evaluate the pros and cons of walking to school.
Let's evaluate the pros and cons of this new game.
Let's evaluate the pros and cons of online shopping.
b2: Let's evaluate the pros and cons of working remotely full-time.
Let's evaluate the pros and cons of outsourcing our technical support.
Let's evaluate the pros and cons of the proposed merger.
Let's evaluate the pros and cons of adopting a new software platform.
Let's evaluate the pros and cons of entering the Asian market.
c1: Let's evaluate the pros and cons of the different theoretical frameworks.
Let's evaluate the pros and cons of the current fiscal policy on long-term growth.
Let's evaluate the pros and cons of decentralized management versus a hierarchical one.
Let's evaluate the pros and cons of prioritizing shareholder value over stakeholder well-being.
Let's evaluate the pros and cons of using genetic engineering in agriculture.

pattern: To ensure success, we must first address ...
translation: 성공을 보장하기 위해서는 먼저 ...을 해결해야 합니다.
b1: To ensure success, we must first address the money problem.
To ensure success, we must first address the time issue.
To ensure success, we must first address the rules.
To ensure success, we must first address the team work.
To ensure success, we must first address the plan.
b2: To ensure success, we must first address the communication gaps within the team.
To ensure success, we must first address the technical limitations of our current infrastructure.
To ensure success, we must first address the concerns raised by our investors.
To ensure success, we must first address the recent decline in customer satisfaction.
To ensure success, we must first address the potential legal issues with the project.
c1: To ensure success, we must first address the fundamental systemic biases in our recruitment process.
To ensure success, we must first address the discrepancy between our vision and our execution.
To ensure success, we must first address the ethical implications of our strategic shift.
To ensure success, we must first address the psychological factors that impede innovation.
To ensure success, we must first address the inherent vulnerabilities in our supply chain.

pattern: The biggest obstacle standing in the way is ...
b1: The biggest obstacle standing in the way is the rain.
The biggest obstacle standing in the way is the high price.
The biggest obstacle standing in the way is the lack of time.
The biggest obstacle standing in the way is my fear.
The biggest obstacle standing in the way is the closed road.
b2: The biggest obstacle standing in the way is the lack of funding for the new department.

The biggest obstacle standing in the way is the lack of skilled personnel in the region.
The biggest obstacle standing in the way is the resistance to change from the management.

The biggest obstacle standing in the way is the complex geopolitical tension in the target market.
The biggest obstacle standing in the way is the inherent fragility of the global financial system.
The biggest obstacle standing in the way is the deep-seated cultural divide among the participants.
The biggest obstacle standing in the way is the absence of a clear ethical framework for the project.

pattern: My proposed approach involves ...
b1: My proposed approach involves a lot of practice.
My proposed approach involves asking for help.
My proposed approach involves working together.

My proposed approach involves the utilization of advanced algorithmic models for prediction.
My proposed approach involves a fundamental restructuring of the organizational hierarchy.
My proposed approach involves a proactive strategy for identifying and mitigating risks.
My proposed approach involves a holistic analysis of both qualitative and quantitative data.

b1: Perhaps the missing piece is the right tool.
